page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz. This single physical core configuration supports hyper-threading with two threads per core. The processor includes an L1d cache of 48 KB, an L2 cache of 2 MB, and a large L3 cache of 260 MB. It features advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions and Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated mathematical and matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The c4-highcpu-2 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, placing in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 operations. However, due to its low core count, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck, with uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth testing in the bottom 10%, and the Passmark memory score also ranking in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highcpu-2 offers a low-cost entry point into Google Cloud's dedicated Xeon-based compute family, trading off memory capacity and multi-threaded throughput for high single-threaded performance. This profile makes it suitable for single-threaded processing tasks, lightweight text processing, basic file compression, and low-traffic web serving where high memory capacity is not required. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, parallel software compilation, or complex multi-threaded workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It provides a single physical core with hyper-threading, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it delivers strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression benchmarks. Conversely, its multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities are limited, placing in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. This resource profile offers a cost-effective option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation but minimal memory, making it best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight text processing, and basic file compression tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and a dedicated Intel Xeon CPU, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs and 4 GB RAM, optimized for single-threaded compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU compute instance delivering strong single-threaded performance and advanced Intel instruction sets for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB RAM
Strong single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for Geekbench single-core
Supports advanced vector and matrix extensions including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Limited memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling due to the 2-vCPU configuration
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight text processing, and compression
